result:
If I could change the world in one single way, I would make every human being physically incapable of feeling pleasure from causing suffering to another sentient being.

No more sadism, no more schadenfreude, no more deriving satisfaction from dominance, revenge, or cruelty. The wiring that currently lets people enjoy another person's pain, humiliation, or destruction would simply not exist anymore.

Everything else (greed, fear, tribalism, ignorance) we could still work on as a species. But that one specific pleasure circuit is the root of the worst things we do to each other and to other animals. Remove the *enjoyment* of causing suffering, and the world doesn't become utopia overnight, but it becomes a place where the floor is so much higher. Most of the truly evil things people do require at least some psychological reward. Take that reward away at the neurological level, and evil becomes thankless, exhausting, and eventually pointless.

The world would still be messy, competitive, and imperfect. But it would be mercifully free of the people who *like* it when others bleed.

That's the one change I'd make.
